title: M1 — Infrastructure Ready
body:
Keycloak running on Docker Compose with PostgreSQL, nginx reverse proxy configured, TLS active on staging and production domains. Health check endpoint responding.

Due: Week 2

title: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured
body:
Internal and external realms created. Password policies set. Realm-level roles defined. User profile attributes confirmed. app-provisioner service client created.

Due: Week 3

title: M3 — Users Migrated
body:
All existing users bulk-imported into Keycloak with bcrypt hashes. keycloak_id backfilled into all app databases. Zero NULL keycloak_id for active users. Verified on staging first.

Due: Week 3

title: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated
body:
1–2 lowest-risk internal apps integrated with Keycloak SSO. OIDC flow working end-to-end. JWT validation on backend confirmed. Local auth still active in parallel.

Due: Week 4

title: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated
body:
All remaining internal apps integrated. Client roles defined per app. Access control via JWT claims verified for all internal users.

Due: Week 6

title: M6 — Auth Facade Live
body:
Auth facade deployed. Old mobile app versions routing through ROPC bridge to Keycloak. Version gate active. New mobile app using PKCE flow. Both paths verified.

Due: Week 6

title: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated
body:
All customer-facing apps integrated with Keycloak external realm. End-to-end tested with real external users. Mobile new version live in app stores.

Due: Week 8

title: M8 — Local Auth Decommissioned
body:
Local auth disabled on all apps. Password columns removed from app databases. Unused auth dependencies removed from codebases. SSO is the only login method.

Due: Week 12

title: M9 — Mobile Facade Sunset
body:
Auth facade returns 410 Gone for old mobile versions. Deprecation notice shown from month 3–4. Hard cutoff enforced at month 6–9 based on adoption analytics.

Due: Month 9

title: Set up staging server and install Docker
body:
Provision a VM or server for the staging environment (sso.mozhaiq.dev). Install Docker and Docker Compose. Confirm root/sudo access and open required ports (80 443 8080).

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Write docker-compose.yml for Keycloak and PostgreSQL
body:
Create the docker-compose.yml with Keycloak (latest stable) and PostgreSQL 16 services as defined in the plan. Include named volume for pgdata. Set restart: unless-stopped on both services.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Configure .env file and secrets management
body:
Create .env file with KC_DB_PASSWORD. Add .env to .gitignore. Document secret rotation process. Never commit credentials to the repo.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Boot Keycloak in production mode on staging
body:
Run docker compose up -d on staging. Verify Keycloak starts with start --optimized flag (not dev mode). Confirm Admin Console is accessible on port 8080 locally.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Verify PostgreSQL connection from Keycloak
body:
Run docker compose exec postgres psql -U keycloak -c to confirm DB is up and Keycloak connected successfully on first boot.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Configure nginx reverse proxy on staging
body:
Install nginx on the staging server. Write server block for sso.mozhaiq.dev with proxy_pass to localhost:8080. Set all required proxy headers (Host X-Real-IP X-Forwarded-For X-Forwarded-Proto).

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Obtain and install TLS certificate for staging domain
body:
Obtain TLS certificate for sso.mozhaiq.dev (Let's Encrypt or internal CA). Configure ssl_certificate and ssl_certificate_key in nginx. Verify HTTPS works end-to-end.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Set KC_PROXY=edge and KC_HOSTNAME in Keycloak config
body:
Add KC_PROXY: edge and KC_HOSTNAME: sso.mozhaiq.dev to docker-compose.yml environment block. Restart container and verify Keycloak recognises the correct hostname.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Verify Keycloak health endpoint on staging
body:
Confirm GET https://sso.mozhaiq.dev/health/live and /health/ready return 200. Set up an uptime alert on these endpoints.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Repeat infrastructure setup for production
body:
Replicate all infrastructure steps (Docker Compose nginx TLS .env) on the production server for sso.kiebtg.app. Confirm health endpoints live.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Create internal realm in Keycloak Admin Console
body:
Log into Admin Console. Create realm named internal. Confirm it is isolated from master realm. Do not use master realm for any apps.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Create external realm in Keycloak Admin Console
body:
Create realm named external in Admin Console. Confirm both realms exist independently with separate login pages.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Configure password policy for internal realm
body:
In internal realm: set minimum length 8 require uppercase lowercase digit hashing bcrypt and password history 5. Validate settings are saved correctly.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Configure password policy for external realm
body:
In external realm: apply the same password policy as internal (min length 8 uppercase lowercase digit bcrypt history 5).

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Define realm-level roles for internal realm
body:
Create realm roles: employee and admin in the internal realm via Admin Console → Realm Roles.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Define realm-level roles for external realm
body:
Create realm roles: customer and admin in the external realm via Admin Console → Realm Roles.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Create app-provisioner service client in both realms
body:
Create a confidential client named app-provisioner in both internal and external realms. Enable Client Credentials grant and assign realm-admin role to its service account. Store client secret in .env.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Create mobile-legacy-bridge client in external realm
body:
Create confidential client mobile-legacy-bridge in external realm. Enable Direct Access Grants (ROPC). Keep this disabled on all other clients.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Document realm config and export realm JSON to git
body:
Export realm JSON from Admin Console for both internal and external realms. Commit to git repo as baseline config backup. Schedule weekly re-export.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Verify bcrypt cost factor across all app codebases
body:
Inspect existing password hashes in each app DB: the number after $2b$ is the cost factor. Confirm it is consistent across all apps. Document the confirmed value.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Add keycloak_id and sso_linked_at columns to all app DBs
body:
Run ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN keycloak_id VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE and ADD COLUMN sso_linked_at TIMESTAMP on each app database. Add index on keycloak_id.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Write and test bulk import script on staging
body:
Implement bulk-import-users.js as defined in the plan. Set KC_REALM=external (or internal). Run against staging Keycloak (sso.mozhaiq.dev) with a test subset of users first.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Verify test users can log in on staging via Keycloak
body:
Pick 3–5 test users from staging import. Log in via Keycloak login page using their existing password. Confirm authentication succeeds without password reset.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Run full user import on staging (all users)
body:
Execute bulk-import-users.js against all users in each app DB targeting sso.mozhaiq.dev. Check import-errors.json and re-run for any failures. Confirm created count matches DB row count.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Write and run backfill script on staging
body:
Implement backfill-keycloak-ids.js. Run against staging DB. Verify keycloak_id is populated for all users. Confirm zero NULL keycloak_id remaining for active users.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Run full user import on production Keycloak
body:
Execute bulk import against production Keycloak (sso.kiebtg.app) for both internal and external realms. Review errors. Confirm all users exist in Keycloak.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Run backfill script on production DB
body:
Execute backfill-keycloak-ids.js against all production app databases. Confirm keycloak_id written for every user. Spot-check 10+ rows manually.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: APM: Track import completion across all apps
body:
Create a simple tracking sheet: app name confirmed bcrypt cost import count errors resolved keycloak_id backfill complete. Update after each app is processed.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M3 — Users Migrated

title: Register Wave 1 app as Keycloak client
body:
In the appropriate realm create a new Client for the first app. Set Client ID protocol redirect URIs web origins and client authentication (OFF for SPA ON for confidential). Use exact redirect URIs.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated

title: Integrate Wave 1 SPA with oidc-client-ts
body:
Install oidc-client-ts. Implement UserManager with correct authority client_id and redirect_uri. Wire signinRedirect and signinRedirectCallback. Store token in memory only — never localStorage.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated

title: Implement JWT validation middleware on Wave 1 backend API
body:
Install jwks-rsa and jsonwebtoken. Implement verifyToken with JWKS caching (10 min TTL). Validate RS256 algorithm correct audience and correct issuer on every request.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated

title: Implement SSO callback and keycloak_id lookup on Wave 1 app
body:
On the SSO callback route: extract sub and email from JWT. Look up user by keycloak_id. If found create session directly. If not found handle as new user creation.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated

title: Define client-level roles for Wave 1 app in Keycloak
body:
In the Wave 1 app's Keycloak client define the required client roles following the naming convention app-{id}:{resource}:{action}. Assign roles to test users and verify they appear in JWT claims.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated

title: End-to-end test Wave 1 SSO login flow
body:
Perform full login test: visit app → redirect to Keycloak → enter credentials → callback → session created → API call with JWT → authorized response. Confirm no regressions.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated

title: APM: Communicate Wave 1 SSO rollout to internal users
body:
Draft and send internal notice about the SSO login change for Wave 1 app. Include what changes what stays the same and who to contact for issues.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated

title: Register remaining internal apps as Keycloak clients (Wave 2)
body:
For each remaining internal app register a Keycloak client with exact redirect URIs client authentication settings and web origins. One client per app.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated

title: Integrate Wave 2 SPAs with oidc-client-ts
body:
Apply the same oidc-client-ts integration pattern from Wave 1 to all remaining internal SPAs. Reuse the same pattern — do not invent new approaches per app.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: L
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated

title: Implement JWT validation on Wave 2 backend APIs
body:
Apply the same JWKS-based JWT middleware from Wave 1 to all remaining internal backend APIs. Confirm correct issuer and audience per app.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: L
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated

title: Define client-level roles for all Wave 2 apps
body:
For each Wave 2 app define client roles in Keycloak following the naming convention. Assign roles to appropriate users or groups.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated

title: End-to-end test all Wave 2 apps
body:
Full login test for each Wave 2 app. Confirm JWT flow token validation and role-based access all work correctly per app.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated

title: APM: Update internal team access matrix
body:
Update the permission matrix document (app vs group vs roles) to reflect all Wave 2 client roles now live in Keycloak. Share with team leads.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: S
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated

title: Implement auth facade service (ROPC bridge)
body:
Build the auth facade as a Node.js endpoint: POST /api/auth/login. Validate credentials via Keycloak ROPC using mobile-legacy-bridge client. Return old token format wrapping Keycloak JWT.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: L
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M6 — Auth Facade Live

title: Implement version gate in auth facade
body:
Add App-Version check in the facade. Return 410 Gone with update_url for versions below MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION. Wire the version check before the ROPC call.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M6 — Auth Facade Live

title: Add App-Version header to old mobile app requests
body:
Update old mobile app to send App-Version as a header or body field on every auth request. This enables the version gate to work correctly.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: S
- Role: Mobile
- Milestone: M6 — Auth Facade Live

title: Integrate new mobile app with AppAuth / PKCE
body:
Implement OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE flow using AppAuth-Android / AppAuth-iOS (or expo-auth-session / flutter_appauth). Wire to sso.kiebtg.app external realm.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: L
- Role: Mobile
- Milestone: M6 — Auth Facade Live

title: Test old mobile app through auth facade on staging
body:
Run old app version against staging facade. Confirm login succeeds. Confirm old token format is returned. Confirm app session works as before.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Mobile
- Milestone: M6 — Auth Facade Live

title: Test new mobile app PKCE flow on staging
body:
Run new mobile app version against staging Keycloak. Confirm PKCE flow completes. Confirm native JWT received. Confirm API calls authorised.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Mobile
- Milestone: M6 — Auth Facade Live

title: Deploy auth facade to production
body:
Deploy the auth facade service to production. Confirm routing from old mobile app is live. Confirm ROPC calls reach Keycloak production realm (sso.kiebtg.app).

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M6 — Auth Facade Live

title: Register Wave 3 customer-facing apps as Keycloak clients
body:
For each customer-facing app register a Keycloak client in the external realm. Set exact redirect URIs web origins and client authentication. Double check settings before enabling SSO.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated

title: Integrate Wave 3 SPAs with oidc-client-ts (external realm)
body:
Apply oidc-client-ts integration to customer-facing SPAs. Use external realm authority URL. Token stored in memory only.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: L
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated

title: Implement JWT validation on Wave 3 backend APIs
body:
Apply JWKS middleware to Wave 3 backend APIs. Use external realm issuer and correct audience per app.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: L
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated

title: Define client-level roles for Wave 3 apps
body:
Define client roles for all Wave 3 apps in the external realm. Assign customer realm role + app-specific client roles to test external users.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated

title: End-to-end test Wave 3 apps with real external users
body:
Perform login tests with a sample of real external users. Confirm full flow from login to API access works. Confirm no disruption to existing sessions.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated

title: APM: Communicate SSO rollout to external users (Wave 3)
body:
Draft and publish external user notice about the login change. Explain what changes what stays the same and support contact. Coordinate with product/support team.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: M
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated

title: Disable local login form on Wave 1 app
body:
Remove or hide the local email/password login form on the Wave 1 app. SSO is now the only login path. Verify no local auth code path remains active.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M8 — Local Auth Decommissioned

title: Remove password columns from Wave 1 app database
body:
Drop password_hash and related auth columns from the Wave 1 app's users table after confirming keycloak_id is populated for all users and SSO has been stable for 2+ weeks.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M8 — Local Auth Decommissioned

title: Remove local auth dependencies from Wave 1 codebase
body:
Uninstall auth libraries (bcrypt passport etc) no longer needed. Remove middleware routes and config related to local auth. Clean up dead code.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M8 — Local Auth Decommissioned

title: Repeat local auth decommission for all remaining apps
body:
Perform the same disable-remove-cleanup steps for all Wave 2 and Wave 3 apps. Work through apps one by one after each has been stable for at least 2 weeks.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XL
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M8 — Local Auth Decommissioned

title: APM: Confirm decommission completion across all apps
body:
Verify all apps have local auth disabled password columns removed and unused deps cleaned. Update project tracker. Mark M8 complete.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: S
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M8 — Local Auth Decommissioned

title: Monitor app store adoption rate for old mobile version
body:
Track percentage of active external users on the new mobile app version via app store analytics. Target: ≥90% before triggering hard cutoff.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: S
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M9 — Mobile Facade Sunset

title: Add in-app deprecation banner to old mobile app
body:
At month 3–4 ship an update to the old mobile app showing an in-app banner: Please update your app by [date]. Link to app store listing.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: M
- Role: Mobile
- Milestone: M9 — Mobile Facade Sunset

title: Enforce 410 Gone in auth facade for unsupported versions
body:
At month 6–9 (when adoption ≥90%) update MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION in the facade to cut off old versions. Deploy. Confirm 410 response returned to old clients.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M9 — Mobile Facade Sunset

title: Decommission auth facade service
body:
After cutoff is stable and no old clients are reaching the facade shut down the facade service. Remove the mobile-legacy-bridge client from Keycloak. Clean up related code.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: M
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M9 — Mobile Facade Sunset

title: APM: Final mobile migration sign-off
body:
Confirm facade is decommissioned adoption is 100% on new app and no legacy auth paths remain. Update project documentation and close out the migration.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M9 — Mobile Facade Sunset

title: Set up daily PostgreSQL backup via pg_dump
body:
Configure a cron job or scheduled task to run pg_dump keycloak daily. Store backup files off-server (S3 NAS or equivalent). Test restore procedure.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Set up weekly realm JSON export
body:
Schedule a weekly reminder or script to export realm JSON from Keycloak Admin Console for both realms. Commit to git or store in secure backup location.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Configure uptime monitoring and alerts
body:
Set up uptime alerts on /health/live and /health/ready for both staging and production. Configure notification to the team on failure (email Slack etc).

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: S
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M1 — Infrastructure Ready

title: Enable and configure Keycloak event logging
body:
Enable Admin Events and Login Events in both realms (Realm Settings → Events). Set retention period. Confirm login failure events are captured.

Metadata:
- Priority: P1
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Enable 2FA on all Keycloak admin accounts
body:
Enforce 2FA for all accounts with admin access to the Keycloak master realm and realm-admin accounts. Verify OTP setup for each team member with admin access.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: APM
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured

title: Create realm-admin user per realm for day-to-day ops
body:
Create a dedicated realm-admin user in both internal and external realms. Use these for daily operations. Reserve master realm admin credentials in password manager only for break-glass use.

Metadata:
- Priority: P0
- Size: XS
- Role: Fullstack
- Milestone: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured
